satire_attempt: Have you ever felt blocked on deprecating your senior architect's auth service? Try the patented stranger-test! Just imagine a stranger built it. Would you delete it? Yes? Congratulations, your respect is fake. The principal whose name appears in the all-hands deck would be proud. Subscribe for next week's verse where the stranger-test is applied to your marriage.
verdict_reason: My satire lands a glancing blow on the 'stranger-test' framing — but it requires inventing claims the verse does not make (that the test is universally applicable, that the senior architect would be 'proud,' that respect is reduced to a single binary). The verse explicitly preserves cases where loyalty IS the right kind ('refusal-as-inquiry') and explicitly preserves cases where the original architect's collaboration is required. The mockery only works against a strawman. PASS.
